steps.title: Identify the core claim
steps.content: The team's conclusion links exposure to distinction ability—specifically, the ability to distinguish familiar languages from unfamiliar ones. A supporting finding must show that dogs with more exposure demonstrate a stronger distinction ability.

steps.title: Match the evidence to the claim
steps.content:
Choice A directly shows this: older dogs—with accumulated exposure over time—display the greatest difference in brain activity between familiar and unfamiliar languages, establishing that more exposure sharpens distinction ability.

Choices B and D reference scrambled non-speech rather than unfamiliar languages, and C shows the distinction pattern is uniform across ages—none support the exposure-ability link.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
